title: Histone extraction for mass spectrometry-based analysis of post-translational modifications in the fungal genus Aspergillus
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"5j71p","text":"Mass spectrometry is a powerful technique to uncover histone post-translational modifications in an unbiased and quantitative manner (Noberini,et al.,The FEBS Journal, 2021). Yet, such analyses require good quality protein samples, a challenge when working with fungi. Here, we present a protocol based on the work by Noberini and colleagues (Noberini, et al., Methods, 2020) to extract histone proteins from filamentous fungi for applications like mass spectrometry-based analyses of post-translational modifications. Several modifications were made: 1) additional cell disruption and homogenization steps using freeze-drying and TissueLyser II; 2) increased Triton X-100 concentration in the lysis buffer; and 3) prolonged incubation time in lysis buffer. This protocol is reproducible, and the proteins extracted from three Aspergillus species (A. niger, A. fumigatus, and A. nidulans) were of good quality for mass spectrometry.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":143,"length":5},{"style":"italic","offset":353,"length":5},{"style":"italic","offset":827,"length":11},{"style":"italic","offset":848,"length":8},{"style":"italic","offset":858,"length":13},{"style":"italic","offset":876,"length":11}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"b0o8s","text":"1. Germinated spores from overnight cultures are the ideal starting material.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5f7bm","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dg5tc","text":"2. Disruption by TissueLyser II and sonication are necessary steps because fungal cells are protected by the cell wall which prevents efficient lysis.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"an2gh","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"21jav","text":"3. 100 mg ground starting material per sample of Aspergillus ends up with enough yield (around 20 µg) for Bradford Protein Assay, SDS-PAGE visualization, and mass spectrometry-based analysis (which ideally requires 3-4 µg of histones). Different fungal species may vary.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":49,"length":11}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"e9fqc","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dmj5v","text":"4. Different medium are used to culture fungal strains, e.g. A. niger and A. fumigatus grow on MEA (Malt Extract Agar) medium at 30 ℃ and 25 ℃, respectively; A. nidulans grows on YAG (Yeast Agar Glucose) medium at 37 ℃.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":61,"length":8},{"style":"italic","offset":74,"length":12},{"style":"italic","offset":158,"length":11}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"59qeb","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

Set centrifuges at 4 ℃ beforehand.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"c16uq","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"f915j","text":"3. Prepare lysis buffers with different Triton X-100 concentration fresh and add PMSF last. PMSF stock solution in isopropanol can be stored at -20 ℃ for several months, but it can be inactivated within 35 minutes once it is added in aqueous solution.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"78i50","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Harvest fungal spores from 4- or 5 -day old sporulating plates with a spreader in 12.5 mL ice-cold ACES buffer
Discard supernatant and resuspend spores in 25 mL ice-cold ACES buffer.
Resuspend spores in 10 mL ice-cold ACES buffer, and count spore concentration with an hemacytometer or spore counter. Ideally, the spore concentration is around 1 × 10 7 spores/ mL.
Inoculate 50 mL of liquid medium with 1 ~ 10 × 10 7 spores in a 250 mL flask, and incubate at 30 ℃, 200 rpm, for 16 hours.
Collect germinated spores by centrifugation at 4,000 xg for 10 minutes and discard supernatant.
Resuspend germinated spore pellet in 25 mL 0.8 M NaCl.
Freeze germinated spores in liquid nitrogen.
Grind freeze-dried samples with a mortar and pestle, and then with TissueLyser II using two ⌀5 mm metal beads at 30 Hz for 30 seconds, which is repeated twice.
Resuspend 100 mg ground sample in 1 mL lysis buffer with high Triton X-100 concentration (1×PBS, 0.5 mM PMSF, 5 µM Leupeptin, 5 µM Aprotinin, 5mM Na-butyrate, and 10% Triton X-100).

Add 10 ml lysis buffer with low Triton X-100 concentration (1×PBS, 0.5 mM PMSF, 5 µM Leupeptin, 5 µM Aprotinin, 5mM Na-butyrate, and 1% Triton X-100), mix by vortexing, and incubate on the orbital shaker at 4 ℃ for another 30 minutes.
Centrifuge at 3,250 xg for 30 minutes at 4 ℃, and discard supernatant.
Add 9 mL ice-cold PBS buffer and mix by vortexing.
Resuspend pellet in 2 mL 0.4 N H 2 SO 4 and incubate at 4 ℃ for 4 hours on an orbital shaker.
Centrifuge at 16,000 xg for 10 minutes at 4 ℃. The supernatant contains soluble core histone proteins.
Add 4 volumes of acetone to the sample, and keep at -20 ℃ overnight to precipitate histone proteins.
Centrifuge at 6,000 xg for 25 minutes at 4 ℃ to pellet proteins, then dissolve in 100 µL water.
Measure protein concentration with the Bradford Protein Assay (He, F., Bio-protocol, 2011).
Sonicate for 30 seconds; this process is repeated 10 times with 30 seconds break in between.
Check protein quality via 16% SDS-PAGE (Sodium dodecyl sulfate polyacrylamide gel electrophoresis) gel (Simpson, R. J., Cold Spring Harbor Protocols, 2006).
Centrifuge the spore suspension at 2,500 xg at 4 ℃ for 5 minutes.
Centrifuge the spore suspension at 2,500 xg at 4 ℃ for 5 minutes, and discard supernatant.
Freeze dry overnight.
Incubate on an orbital shaker for 30 minutes at 4 ℃.
Centrifuge at 4,000 xg for 10 minutes and discard supernatant to get rid of liquid medium.
Centrifuge at 3,250 xg for 20 minutes at 4 ℃, and discard supernatant.
Resuspend in 1 mL ice-cold PBS buffer.
creator: Xin Zhang
